reviews.review_msg: I really enjoyed this class, though it is my interest. He has a clear passion for what he does, and is always willing to help you. I'd take another class with him NO PROBLEM! In fact, I am! Text wasn't used all that much, just take good notes and pay attention and you'll be fine!
Amazing professor, really kind as well. Lectures and assignments are very clear and workload is very balanced. Waived an assignment that I forgot to do.

She honestly deserves a 5/5- I've never met a prof like her. She actually cares about her students and their success. She makes a science course easy to understand- especially for non-science related majors. Her lectures are simple and she ensures that every complicated aspect to it is understandable. Climate change memo was lowkey fun.
The professor is awesome. Course didn't require much time and was fairly easy. The only hard part was the climate change solutions memo, which caused a lot of people's grades to drop.

I took GEOG 104 as a breadth science course. Every week Professor Holland would assign us a list of things we needed to do; discussion post, short assignment, and every other week a 15 question openbook quiz. I recommend this to everyone as a doable and easy class. I took it during the Summer intersession and spent only 6-7 hours a week.
Very difficult marker and is extremely particular on what she is looking for. Lowest grade I have received in my 3 years of university.
Dr. Holland was an amazing professor, and her class was set up really nice I actually feel like I walked away learning something. Definitely take a class with her.

This was an easy and interesting class MOSTLY because of the professor. She genuinely cares about her students and wants to teach in a way that keeps students engaged- even as an online course. She is an absolute angel. 10/10 prof.
easiest class you'll ever take

Tara is an amazing professor. The content she taught was genuinely interesting and her lectures were extremely easy to follow! The assignments were easy to do. However, I just wish the TA's would provide a bit more feedback for assignments. Please take a course with Tara she is so passionate about what she teaches, it really rubs off on you!

I thought I was the only one with a bad experience with the memo. I worked so hard for an A+ just for the memo to drop me to an A. Prof made it clear there were no regrades for that too... I wonder if it was because grades were too high and they had to find some way to drop everyone's mark down. Not fair imo for those who worked hard

Weekly mundane busy work to get through, though not very difficult at all. TAs are terrible at marking and will ruin your grade with the memo if it isn't anything less than perfect. Lectures are insightful for the most part and course resources are well organized.

Class is meh, lots of reflections and personal connections have to be made. TA's absolutely ruin your grade at the end and show no mercy marking the final synthesis memo. Be ready to drop a letter grade if you didn't put in like 10 hours completing the assignment. Tara is respectful, but TA's ruined it for me...
TAs are horrible markers. Do well on everything during the semester so when they mark your memo you don't drop a whole letter grade. Tough markers.

Her course Canvas page is very organized making it easy to stay on top of things. The class is very straight forward and some of/a lot of it is common sense. There is an optional textbook, but I didn't even bother buying it and there were no questions on tests or quizzes on it. Overall, a good class.

Tara is amazing! One of the best lecturers I've had yet. Course work was fair given that it was intersession, but TAs weren't the best at giving feedback that reflected your grade. Tests were very straightforward too.

This is by far the best online class! So well organized you cannot help but succeed if you follow along in a timely manner. The text book is nice to have but not necessary. Tara really cares about the environment and her students. Marking is fair and quick. Highly recommend this class.
Quizzes are easy, and quiet many assignments to do. Tara is also nice and friendly! The worst about GEOG104 - TA are tough marker...
really enjoyed this class and would def recommend it as a GPA booster!

Great prof, can tell she really cares and wants students to understand the content. Lots of participation assignments but were mostly just for completion. I'm not great at science but found the prof made the content easy to understand. Very understanding considering quarantine as well!
Great prof. Enjoyed the course and was taught well. Would highly recommend to another student.

Not really a GPA booster as the TA's are picky with the assignments. Tests and quizzes are easy as long as u go over the material. Anywho why does it matter global warming is not real anyways. Tara was a solid prof tho but since global warming is fake the class is fake.

Super organized and responds to emails really fast which made online learning so nice. Her quizzes and exam were exactly what you would expect given the material. If taken online, I highly recommend going to the optional Zoom Q&As to really solidify your learning. Overall, she's a great teacher who is very caring and easy to talk to.
Good online class for grades

Highly recommend Geog 104 with this prof. Clear guidelines, weekly modules to follow along and the work was very manageable. Final assignment was also quite manageable to do. Definitely a gpa booster take this class with this prof if you can!

Responds quickly to questions, is organized, and doesn't demand too much of you. Most lectures were actually done using Tara Holland's slides and video lectures, I preferred Pan's tho. Minimal readings, some videos to watch and respond to. Good course to take for an elective. Even gave us a week off in the middle of the sem.

Highly recommend this course if you need a science credit for the breath requirement. It's definitely one of the more manageable science options and it's hard to get a bad grade as long as you finish the assignments on time.

Probably my favourite professor. GEOG 104 was sometimes a bit dull in content, but it was designed to help you relate to your place in the world. You could choose a topic you wanted to research, which made the assignments more interesting. She genuinely cares about students' learning, and you can see that in her teaching style.

He really tried, and I'm sure he's a nice person but the course was really dry (lol no pun intended). I don't think he was the problem, I think it was that good-awful TA who literally deducted points for font size on a poster. Other than that, no textbook and relatively easy concepts, for some reason though, people didn't really do well.
easy courses with weekly quizzes and assignments, but it takes 48hrs for the prof to reply to my email about the final project, cuz the instructions are bit confused and unclear.
Weekly lecture videos and discussion/activities that take around an hour to do. The projects and exams were very straightforward. Lecture videos are also nice and short.

Leanne is a very flexible prof with a 3 day extension on every other assignment. Each week there was one very short lecture and a simple quiz after. One thing that sucked was the marking. The TA's were sometimes very picky and for the poster project your group members decided your mark and you can't do anything about it.

Her online class was very well-structured and clearly laid out. She marked fairly and the assignments were straight-forward. Only thing I didn't really like was the novel study, but she gave ample time between check-ins so it wasn't too bad. Overall would recommend this course!

Course was pretty easy and straightforward, especially with no final, however, don't expect an A+ or anything because that is virtually impossible due to the small rooms for error you can have on some of the heavily weighted assesments
P.S the textbook readings are useless i didn't open it once
Leanne was a good prof and the course was not overly difficult. She was also very accommodating.

Honestly surprised at how low her rating is !! You have to read the course material and watch the lectures to do well in this class, but all the material is interesting so it's not too difficult. Dr. Roderick also goes out of her way to make the course better for students (ex. changing things about the tests after student feedback).
Easy and light class with 2 major assignments, just watch lectures and memorize the concept and you're good to go

It was an easy class overall, with only a couple big assignments. I DID NOT read a textbook at all and did not go to optional zoom meetings. Just watched her slides, it was enough.

A few assignments took a while to write so make sure to start early. She gave us enough time (even too much) to do them. The easiest class I have even taken.

Had to take this class to fulfill my degree-related social science/science breadth requirements as part of graduation. By far, my worst class grade-wise this term given how awful this prof was since she expected the students to come in with 300-400 level stuff knowledge.

Pretty straightforward course, as long as you review lecture slides and study the learning goals you will do fine. Dr. Roderick was an amazing professor, she was caring and even gave us a 1% bonus for completing the course review survey. Assignments weren't too difficult and I learned a lot about our climate in this course. Highly Recommend!

Content is straightforward/prof is passionate and caring. TAs grade harshly sometimes, just focus on meeting criteria in rubrics and ur good. I got the textbook but didn't do any readings b/c lecture content overlapped with them. Do the other readings/short vids tho! Study for quizzes/final using weekly learning goals and you'll get an easy A.

Lots of smaller assignments that are effort-based to help ensure you understand the big concepts. The prof was understanding whenI found myself in a tight spot with meeting deadlines.  A lot of what people are complaining about are beyond the prof's control - e.g. an online exam for 400 students would crash the Canvas server.

Took this course as an elective thinking it'd be easy for a 100 level but it's my worst class this sem because I couldn't understand anything. Leanne reads off the slides, not explaining concepts in depth. Worked hard on my poster project and got 50% on it without proper feedback. Also an in person final for an online class at 7-10pm is insane !!

Dr. Roderick is super nice and seems to genuinely care about her students. However, the lectures were uninformative and she just read off slides. Most issues (large class size of 400, and inconsistent marking by TAs) are genuinely out of her control for the most part. She's a great person but it's unfortunate that the course was not.

This course is meant to be easy as 100 level, but I will add on to other people, she along with her TAs confused us so much. To this day, I do not know what she or TAs expect from us, so lost studying for the exam. The readings and videos all seem to cover the same idea but are phrased differently, don't know how much detail I have to memorize.

They expect us to know 400 level stuff and marked my assignments acc to that, I do not know who gave her TEACHING EXCELLENCE AWARD, I mean, she reads off Tara Holland's slides. Is that reasonable? This GPA booster took more of my time as compared to my 400 level business courses, PLEASE DO NOT TAKE THIS PROF OR THE COURSE, TAKE OTHER COURSES!!!

WORST PROFESSOR EVER! If you think this will be a GPA booster, please rethink. Worst prof, worst course, hell TAs. Do not take this course ever! 3 major assignments and the grading was unclear, TAs expect us to know 300-400 level stuff that we are supposed to know for a 100 level course, I mean, al least be reasonable!!!!

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS AS A GPA BOOSTER. You will not get above 80%. So many articles and textbook pages to read WEEKLY. Absolutely atrocious. An online class with an in-person exam that is cumulative. Terrible terrible TERRIBLE. STAY AWAYYYYYYYYY

Tedious class with so many little things due every week on top of lectures, alongside unclear grading criteria. It's my last semester, and this is one of the most tedious workloads I have ever had for a class. Prof introduces assignments in the week it's due, which is not helpful at all when you are balancing other courses. Would not recommend!

Weekly class engagement act. were for 1% but considering how tedious they were, should have been like 30%. Quizzes were so hard, I studied for the whole week and still got 8/11. Unclear questions and 'choose all that apply' made it worse. Never recommend class with this professor, please don't think its a gpa booster because it is not!!

This "GPA-boosting" elective has consumed so much of my time from the weekly participation, quizzes and assignments, more than my upper-level classes. The lecture is useless. Quizzes feel tedious. The canvas page is so painful to go through. TAs are hard markers. Textbook readings + many mini articles + engagements are assigned every. week.

Worst class I've ever taken at SFU (I'm in 3rd year). Everything was graded super unfairly, the class has so many people that the TAs don't wanna deal with people complaining about marks. I don't know why people say this is an easy A! Such ridiculous assignments (and a lot of them)! Unorganized class, wrong dates on canvas, I could go on...

The professor for this class directly reads off the slides and uses all the course material by Dr. Tara Holland. I understand running a course can be difficult but atleast not read directly off the slides or have so many things due each week. Since this class is online yet we have an in-person final exam covering the whole course.

Enthusiastic and engaging professor. However, the course has too many little things due every week on top of lectures to watch and readings which makes the class take more time and work and the final is in-person for an online class.

Dr. Roderick is very nice, makes lectures straightforward and the assignments were fun and interactive. Took this class for WQB credit and really enjoyed it. Definitely recommend Dr. Roderick and the course :)

The course is fairly easy for ppl who don't have science background (like me) as long as you complete all the work. Workload is not that heavy, but is broken down in many little things sometimes.
The prof is accessible through canvas discussion, and she replied in a decent amount of time (sometimes it took her longer).
A lot of homework and in the assignment/homework, you could lost some points for "ridiculous" reason. also she assume that the class know "everything" and dont event talk about that topic

104 is a super cool course. Super little reading, just watch lecture videos and go to class, low workload relative to some other courses i have taken. And you get to learn abt climate change, important subject fr.
Terrible professor, NEVER take her course again

Took the ASYNC course, presentations are very clear, recorded lectures are short, wasn't too difficult to keep up with, although there was a lot of homework assigned weekly (pretty easy though). Would recommend for both required course and elective.

Takes long to respond to emails and sometimes won't respond at all. When she does, little help is actually provided. Grading rubrics are very vague and I often got no feedback about marks lost. Never received final exam mark and am unaware of how I got the mark I did. 2nd project marked CONSIDERABLY harder than 1st. Many grammar errors in slides.

This course had a heavy course load but the marking was very fair. The prof is very fair with her marking as long as you work for it. Many students didnt reach out to her and ended up getting a bad grade on their final group project. This class is great as long as you keep in touch with the prof!

We had an open book midterm and final. Quizzes/Assignments were okay but really time-consuming and her expectations varied at times, sometimes she'd mark super easy and other times more harshly. Our first group project was marked fairly easy, where as the last group project was marked very unfairly and dropped the entire class average.

This class was online and has a test each week and an assignment plus 2 group projects and a midterm and final. so lots of work, the thing that sucks is her grading rubrics given to you before and after projects may not match. She doesn't respond quickly to questions and you cant contact TA.

sharla is nice and enjoys teaching but she doesn't give enough time for quizzes. the whole course is basically just assignments. you won't hear back from her after 2-3 days and she doesn't really answer questions. fairly easy. the grading for assignments is a little wack. just make sure you do the lectures and assignments. can't contact ta's either
